channel.title: Comments for page "I'll Be Home For Christmas..."
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "I'll Be Home For Christmas..."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not the most in-depth thing in the world, but this works well to show the comedic hypocrisy and bureaucracy of the Three Moons Initiative, and I'd say it even does a good job of introducing the GoI and Corbenic for first-time readers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll be honest, the Three Moons Initiative has always made me viscerally uncomfortable, and this is a good example of why. Everything we're shown about them indicate that their society is a Kafka-esque atrocity where minor crimes are given horrifying punishments, where torture is indulged in, and without regard for the safety of others (I'm thinking of the SCP where the Three Moons people are in various fiction, and in pornographic fiction they're standing around with rocket launchers to make sure the sex is consensual; of course such a weapon could not be used without killing the victim).

But we're never shown the actual consequences of their behavior, since as far as I can tell they're only shown going after villains. It's like in cop shows where the cops constantly commit gross abuses of power and miscarriages of justice, but somehow they're always right so therefore it's okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not entirely a fan of this - it feels more like the B-plot to a larger tale, but I'm thankful that it addresses the issues with lying to the agents. Overall, a tentative +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If he needs help getting out of hell, I've got a helpline he can call.
